question.title: Identify Consecutive Logins
question.desc: Given a table of user logins, identify users who have logged in consecutively for at least 3 days.
question.solution: WITH consecutive_logins AS ( SELECT user_id, login_date, DATEADD('day', -ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY login_date), login_date) AS group_id FROM logins ) SELECT user_id, MIN(login_date) AS start_date, MAX(login_date) AS end_date FROM consecutive_logins GROUP BY user_id, group_id HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3;
question.tips: Consider using window functions to efficiently handle sequences.
hints: Consider using window functions to identify consecutive sequences
Look into using ROW_NUMBER() or RANK() to assign a sequence identifier to each row
Use a Common Table Expression (CTE) to break down the problem into manageable parts

tests.setup: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS logins; CREATE TABLE logins (user_id INT, login_date DATE); INSERT INTO logins (user_id, login_date) VALUES (4, '2023-02-01'), (4, '2023-02-02'), (4, '2023-02-03'), (5, '2023-02-01'), (5, '2023-02-02');

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS logins; CREATE TABLE logins (user_id INT, login_date DATE); INSERT INTO logins (user_id, login_date) VALUES (6, '2024-03-01'), (6, '2024-03-02'), (6, '2024-03-03'), (6, '2024-03-04'), (7, '2024-03-01'), (7, '2024-03-03');
